mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation creates a supply-demand imbalance that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive GDP and CPI growth periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 58% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of expansion periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 2.0%, borrowing costs for corporations rise sharply, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or signal loses predictive power after 2018.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightness that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observed instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and consumer confidence, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS and HOUST growth periods fail to outperform baseline returns, with 60-day positive return frequency dropping below 50% across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt manufacturing contraction and supply chain disruptions, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when industrial production drops below the -0.5% threshold, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.
